The housekeeping manager oversees 100 rooms and manages daily concerns. The number of rooms requiring attention follows a Poisson distribution with a mean of 20. The concierge assists with 40% of these concerns.  

Module 1: Calculate the probability that exactly 15 rooms need attention on a given day using the Poisson distribution.  

Module 2: Determine the probability that at least 7 of 8 concerns are resolved successfully using the binomial distribution.  

Module 3: Apply the Poisson distribution to find the probability of exactly 15 rooms needing attention.  

Module 4: Use the binomial distribution to find the probability of at least 7 resolved concerns out of 8.  

The housekeeping manager ensures that 20% of rooms need attention on average. The concierge resolves 90% of the concerns they handle.  

The probability of exactly 15 rooms needing attention is calculated using the Poisson formula. The probability of at least 7 resolved concerns is calculated using the binomial formula.  

The probability of exactly 15 rooms needing attention is 0.0723. The probability of at least 7 resolved concerns is 0.8147.  

The housekeeping manager ensures that 20% of rooms need attention on average. The concierge resolves 90% of the concerns they handle.
